items.summary: Manchester United have agreed a deal to sign Atalanta midfielder Ederson.

items.title: Aston Villa intent on keeping Rogers - Wednesday's gossip
items.summary: Aston Villa do not plan to sell Morgan Rogers amid interest in the attacking midfielder, Barcelona remain keen on signing Marcus Rashford from Manchester United and Real Madrid hold talks with Inter Milan right-back Denzel Dumfries.

items.title: ‘Football retirements hit differently’ joke fans as Eden Hazard becomes face of shock new product in time for World Cup
items.summary: EDEN HAZARD now has a burger named after him - and it's just in time for the start of the World Cup. items.title: Man Utd reach agreement to sign Atalanta midfielder Ederson, 26, on five-year deal worth £39million
items.summary: MANCHESTER UNITED have reached an agreement with Atalanta to sign midfielder Ederson. The 26-year-old Brazilian heads to Old Trafford in a four-year deal worth up to £39million. items.title: Fulham confirm Silva to leave club this summer
items.summary: Fulham have confirmed that Marco Silva will be leaving the club this summer after a five-year stint at Craven Cottage.

items.title: Sciver-Brunt 'ready to go' for T20 World Cup - but 'probably' won't bowl
items.summary: England head coach Charlotte Edwards says captain Nat Sciver-Brunt is "ready to go" for the home T20 World Cup - but unlikely to bowl.

items.summary: Martin O'Neill and Robbie Keane are reportedly the main contenders to become Celtic manager next season.

items.title: England players will be ready for heat, says Henderson
items.summary: Jordan Henderson urges England's players to expose themselves to the sweltering Florida conditions so they can acclimatise to the intense heat expected at the World Cup.

items.title: Salford sack Robinson after play-off final loss
items.summary: Salford City manager Karl Robinson is sacked by the League Two club nine days after their play-off final defeat.

items.title: EFL manager, 45, SACKED despite leading club to their best ever season after play-offs heartbreak
items.summary: SALFORD have sacked manager Karl Robinson. The former MK Dons, Charlton and Oxford boss has been axed despite leading the club to their best-ever season. items.summary: Scotland's players have not discussed boycotting their World Cup qualifying double header against Israel, says midfielder Christy Grimshaw.

items.title: Fury as thousands of pubs face having to kick out England fans before end of World Cup final if it goes to penalties
items.summary: THOUSANDS of pubs face having to kick out England fans before the end of the World Cup final if it goes to penalties, The Sun can reveal. items.title: Source: Iran expects U.S., Mexico visas this week
items.summary: With their first match of the 2026 World Cup less than two weeks away, Iran are expecting to receive visas to enter Mexico on Wednesday, followed by entry visas to the United States on Friday, a source in direct contact with Team Melli told ESPN.

items.title: U.S. delays WCup entry for Swiss striker Embolo
items.summary: Switzerland forward Breel Embolo was delayed flying to the United States for the World Cup on Tuesday because his travel documents were under review.

items.title: FIFA sued for painting over Dallas whale mural
items.summary: An artist sued FIFA on Monday for $25 million, alleging that the federation and its partners painted over an iconic mural in downtown Dallas without his consent.

items.title: Congo pre-WC friendly canceled over Ebola fears
items.summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo's pre-World Cup friendly against Chile, due to be played in Spain, has been cancelled because of health concerns regarding the Ebola outbreak in the African country.
